docs.title: Dataset Runs
docs.text:
Record and retrieve LLM outputs produced over a dataset.

A dataset run captures the actual model outputs and scores from one evaluation pass over a dataset's items, so you can compare results across different models, prompts, or configurations over time.

Access via sdk.dataset_runs or standalone via Evaluate.dataset_run.

Example 
import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

sdk = AISDK(
    public_key=os.environ["AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["AISDK_SECRET_KEY"],
)
run = sdk.dataset_runs.create(
    dataset_name="customer-qa-dataset",
    name="gpt-4o-baseline",
)
items = sdk.dataset_runs.list_items(
    dataset_name="customer-qa-dataset",
    dataset_run_id=run["id"],
)

Create a new dataset run.

The name of the dataset to create a run for.

required **kwargs

Optional keyword arguments:

  • tag (str) — Tag to attach (only valid for MUTABLE dataset runs).
{}

A dict containing the created run's data, including its id.

Raises:

Type Description ValidationError

If dataset_name is empty or tag is invalid.

AuthenticationError

If the API key is invalid or expired.

TestOpsAPIError

If the API request fails.

Example 
import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

# Via AISDK instance
sdk = AISDK(
    public_key=os.environ["AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["AISDK_SECRET_KEY"],
)
run = sdk.dataset_runs.create(
    dataset_name="customer-qa-dataset",
    name="gpt-4o-baseline",
    tag="v1-baseline",
)
print(run["id"])     # e.g. "run_a1b2c3d4e5f6"
print(run["status"]) # "OPEN"

import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Evaluate

# Standalone (credentials from environment variables)
# Set AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY and AISDK_SECRET_KEY in your environment first
run = Evaluate.dataset_run.create(
    dataset_name="customer-qa-dataset",
    name="claude-3-5-sonnet-run",
    tag="v2-sonnet",
)
print(run["id"])

list(dataset_name, **kwargs)

List dataset runs for a dataset.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default dataset_name str

The name of the dataset.

required **kwargs

Optional keyword arguments:

  • page (int) — Page number for pagination. Defaults to 1.
  • limit (int) — Number of results per page. Defaults to 50.

{}

Raises:

Type Description ValidationError

TestOpsAPIError

If the API request fails.

Example 
import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

# Via AISDK instance
sdk = AISDK(
    public_key=os.environ["AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["AISDK_SECRET_KEY"],

docs.title: Datasets
docs.text:
Manage named datasets used as evaluation input.

A dataset is a versioned collection of input–expected-output rows that experiment runs pull from. Supports creating, listing, and uploading items to datasets.

Access via sdk.datasets on an AISDK instance, or standalone via Evaluate.dataset.

Example 
import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

sdk = AISDK(
    public_key=os.environ["AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["AISDK_SECRET_KEY"],
)
dataset = sdk.datasets.create(name="customer-qa-dataset")
print(dataset["id"])

docs.title: create_items
docs.text:
create_items(dataset_name, items=None, file_url=None, options=None)

Upload multiple items to a dataset in configurable batches.

Items can be supplied directly as a list of dicts, or read from a CSV file. The CSV file must have a header row; column names are mapped directly to item fields (input, expectedOutput, context, etc.).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default dataset_name str

The name of the target dataset.

required items Optional[List[dict]]

List of item dicts. Each dict may contain:

  • input (any): LLM input.
  • expectedOutput (any): Expected result.
  • context (any, optional): Additional context.
  • metadata (any, optional): Arbitrary metadata.
  • id (str, optional): Optional stable item ID.
  • sourceTraceId (str, optional): Linked trace ID.
  • sourceObservationId (str, optional): Linked observation ID.
  • status (str, optional): Item status.

Defaults to None.

None file_url Optional[str]

Local filesystem path to a UTF-8 CSV file containing dataset items. Mutually exclusive with items. Defaults to None.

None options Optional[dict]

Optional configuration dict. Supported key:

  • batchSize (int): Number of items per API call. Defaults to 100.
None

Returns:

Type Description CreateDatasetItemResponse

A dict with success (bool) and itemCount (int) fields.

Raises:

Type Description ValidationError

If neither items nor file_url is provided.

ValueError

If the CSV file cannot be read or parsed.

AuthenticationError

If the API key is invalid or expired.

TestOpsAPIError

If any batch API request fails.

Example 
import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

# Via AISDK instance
sdk = AISDK(
    public_key=os.environ["AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["AISDK_SECRET_KEY"],
)
response = sdk.datasets.create_items(
    dataset_name="customer-qa-dataset",
    items=[
        {
            "input": "What is BrowserStack used for?",
            "expectedOutput": "BrowserStack is a cloud testing platform for web and mobile apps.",
        },
        {
            "input": "Does BrowserStack support real devices?",
            "expectedOutput": "Yes, BrowserStack provides access to 3000+ real devices.",
        },
    ],
)
print(response["itemCount"])  # 2

import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Evaluate

# Standalone — upload from a CSV file
# Set AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY and AISDK_SECRET_KEY in your environment first
response = Evaluate.dataset.create_items(
    dataset_name="customer-qa-dataset",
    file_url="/data/eval_items.csv",
)
print(response["itemCount"])  # number of rows uploaded

docs.title: Evaluation Execution
docs.text:
A lightweight, on-demand API for scoring LLM outputs against quality evaluators — no dataset, experiment, or run configuration required.

Send a single input–output pair with a list of metric evaluators to the BrowserStack Evals API and receive structured scores back in real time. Ideal for CI pipelines, ad-hoc debugging, and one-off quality spot-checks.

Access via sdk.evals or standalone via Evaluate.evaluation_execution.

Example 
import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

sdk = AISDK(
    public_key=os.environ["AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["AISDK_SECRET_KEY"],
)
result = sdk.evals.evaluate({
    "evaluators": [{"metricName": "answer_relevance"}],
    "data": {"input": "What is AI?", "output": "Artificial Intelligence"},
})
print(result["jobId"])

docs.title: evaluate
docs.text:
evaluate(request_data)

Execute an AI quality evaluation against the BrowserStack Evals API.

Sends request_data to the /api/public/evals endpoint and returns structured evaluation results. The request is validated locally before any network call is made.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default request_data EvaluationExecutionRequest

A dict with the following keys:

  • evaluators (list, required): List of evaluator configuration dicts. Each dict must contain metricName (str) and the required params.

  • data (dict, required): The data to evaluate. Each data dict supports:

  • input (str, optional): The user query or prompt.

  • output (str, optional): The model's response.
  • context (list[str], optional): Supporting context passages used to generate the response.
  • expectedOutput (str, optional): Ground-truth answer for correctness metrics.
  • retrievedContexts (list[str], optional): Context chunks retrieved by the RAG pipeline (used by RAG evaluators).
  • referenceContexts (list[str], optional): Ground-truth context documents for context recall metrics.

  • rubrics (dict, optional): Custom scoring rubric for rubric-based evaluators.

  • provider (str, optional): LLM provider to use for the evaluation judge model (e.g. 'openai').

  • model (str, optional): Specific judge model name (e.g. 'gpt-4o').
  • concurrency (int, optional): Number of parallel evaluations (1–10). Defaults to the server default.
required

Returns:

Type Description EvaluationExecutionResponse

A dict with success (bool), results (list of per-evaluator score objects), jobId (str), and timestamp (ISO-8601 str).

Raises:

Type Description ValidationError

If request_data is malformed, evaluators is empty or missing required fields, data is absent, or concurrency is outside the 1–10 range.

EvaluationExecutionError

If the API request fails, the response status code is non-2xx, or the response body does not match the expected schema.

Example 
import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

# Via AISDK instance
sdk = AISDK(
    public_key=os.environ["AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["AISDK_SECRET_KEY"],
)
result = sdk.evals.evaluate({
    "evaluators": [
        {"metricName": "answer_relevance"},
        {"metricName": "faithfulness"},
    ],
    "data": {
        "input": "What is BrowserStack used for?",
        "output": "BrowserStack is a cloud testing platform for web and mobile apps.",
        "context": [
            "BrowserStack provides real device and browser testing in the cloud.",
            "It supports automated and manual testing across 3000+ devices.",
        ],
    },
})
print(result["success"])   # True
for score in result["results"]:
    print(score["metricName"], score["score"])
# answer_relevance  0.95
# faithfulness      0.88

import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Evaluate

# Standalone (credentials from environment variables)
# Set AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY and AISDK_SECRET_KEY in your environment first
result = Evaluate.evaluation_execution.evaluate({
    "evaluators": [
        {"metricName": "answer_relevance"},
        {"metricName": "faithfulness"},
    ],
    "data": {
        "input": "What is BrowserStack used for?",
        "output": "BrowserStack is a cloud testing platform for web and mobile apps.",
        "context": [
            "BrowserStack provides real device and browser testing in the cloud.",
            "It supports automated and manual testing across 3000+ devices.",
        ],
    },
})
print(result["success"])   # True
print(result["jobId"])     # "job_a1b2c3d4"

docs.title: Evaluator Lists
docs.text:
A named, reusable collection of quality evaluators — each pre-configured with scoring parameters — that can be attached to any experiment.

Evaluator lists eliminate per-run metric setup: define your suite of evaluators (e.g. answer relevance, faithfulness, BLEU) once, then reference the list ID when creating experiments for consistent, repeatable scoring across every run.

Access via sdk.evaluator_lists or standalone via Evaluate.evaluator_list.

Example 
# Via AISDK instance
sdk = AISDK(public_key="pk", secret_key="sk")
evl = sdk.evaluator_lists.create({
    "name": "accuracy-suite",
    "evaluators": [{
        "evaluatorId": "ev-abc",
        "params": [{"key": "threshold", "dataType": "float", "value": "0.8"}],
    }],
})
print(evl["id"])

# Standalone
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Evaluate
evl = Evaluate.evaluator_list.create({...})

docs.title: Experiment Runs
docs.text:
Execute and monitor evaluation runs within an experiment.

Each run processes the experiment's linked dataset through its evaluators and records per-item scores. Use subscribe() to poll until the run reaches a terminal state (COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED).

Access via sdk.experiment_runs or standalone via Evaluate.experiment_run.

import asyncio
import os

)

status, data = asyncio.run(sdk.experiment_runs.subscribe(run["id"], timeout=300))
print(status)  # "COMPLETED"

docs.title: subscribe
docs.text:
subscribe(experiment_run_id, timeout=120.0, poll_interval=5.0)

Poll an experiment run until it reaches a terminal state.

This is the primary way to wait for an asynchronous experiment run to finish. It polls the experiment run status at regular intervals using asyncio to avoid blocking the event loop.

Terminal states: 'COMPLETED', 'FAILED', 'CANCELLED', 'TIMEOUT'.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default experiment_run_id str

The unique ID of the experiment run to monitor.

required timeout Optional[float]

Maximum seconds to wait before raising TimeoutError. Pass None for no timeout. Defaults to 120.0 (2 minutes).

120.0 poll_interval float

Seconds to wait between consecutive API polls. Defaults to 5.0.

5.0

Returns:

Type Description Tuple[str, ExperimentRunResponse]

A (final_status, experiment_run_data) tuple where final_status is one of the terminal state strings and experiment_run_data is the final run data dict.

Raises:

Type Description ValidationError

If experiment_run_id is empty or timeout is not a positive number.

ExperimentRunNotFoundError

If the experiment run does not exist.

TimeoutError

If the run does not reach a terminal state within timeout seconds.

AuthenticationError

If the API key is invalid or expired.

AuthorizationError

If the caller lacks permission.

TestOpsAPIError

If any API request fails.

Example 
import asyncio
import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

# Via AISDK instance
sdk = AISDK(
    public_key=os.environ["AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["AISDK_SECRET_KEY"],
)
run = sdk.experiment_runs.create(experiment_id="exp_f1e2d3c4", concurrency=5)
status, data = asyncio.run(
    sdk.experiment_runs.subscribe(run["id"], timeout=300, poll_interval=10)
)
print(status)   # "COMPLETED"
print(data["id"]) # "er_a1b2c3d4"

import asyncio
import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Evaluate

# Standalone
# Set AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY and AISDK_SECRET_KEY in your environment first
run = Evaluate.experiment_run.create(experiment_id="exp_f1e2d3c4")
status, data = asyncio.run(
    Evaluate.experiment_run.subscribe(run["id"], timeout=600, poll_interval=15)
)
if status == "COMPLETED":
    print("Evaluation finished successfully:", data["id"])
elif status == "FAILED":
    print("Evaluation failed:", data.get("error"))

docs.title: Prompt
docs.text:
Manage versioned prompt templates stored in TestOps.

Use get() to fetch a prompt by name, create() to publish a new version, and list() to browse stored prompts. Call .compile() on the object returned by get() to render the final string.

docs.title: get
docs.text:
get(name, *, version=None, label=None, type='text', cache_ttl_seconds=None, fallback=None, max_retries=None, fetch_timeout_seconds=None)

Fetch a prompt and return it as a compilable PromptClient.

Retrieves the prompt from the BrowserStack API, injects id and modelParams metadata onto the returned object, and populates the local prompt cache.

Can be called on an AISDK instance (sdk.prompt.get(...)) or as a standalone classmethod (Prompt.get(...)). When neither version nor label is provided the API resolves the 'latest' label.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default name str

Name of the prompt to retrieve. Must be a non-empty string.

required version Optional[int]

Specific version number to fetch. Defaults to None (resolved via label).

None label Optional[str]

Label to fetch (e.g. 'production'). Defaults to None (falls back to 'latest').

None type Literal['chat', 'text']

Expected prompt type — 'text' or 'chat'. Defaults to 'text'.

'text' cache_ttl_seconds Optional[int]

Local in-memory cache TTL in seconds. Defaults to None (no cache TTL override).

None fallback Union[Optional[List[ChatMessageDict]], Optional[str]]

Fallback content used when the fetch fails. Defaults to None.

None max_retries Optional[int]

Maximum retry attempts on transient failures. Defaults to None (uses SDK default).

None fetch_timeout_seconds Optional[int]

Per-request timeout in seconds. Defaults to None.

None

Returns:

Type Description PromptClient

A PromptClient with the prompt data. Call prompt.compile(variables={...}) to render the final string.

Raises:

Type Description ValidationError

If name is empty or invalid.

AuthenticationError

If the API key is invalid or expired.

TestOpsAPIError

If the API request fails.

Example 
import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

# Via AISDK instance
sdk = AISDK(
    public_key=os.environ["AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["AISDK_SECRET_KEY"],
)
prompt = sdk.prompt.get("customer-support-reply", label="production")
rendered = prompt.compile(variables={"ticket_text": "My order hasn't arrived."})
# rendered → "You are a helpful support agent. Summarise the issue and reply: My order hasn't arrived."

# Fetch a specific version by number
prompt_v2 = sdk.prompt.get("customer-support-reply", version=2)
rendered_v2 = prompt_v2.compile(variables={"ticket_text": "I received the wrong item."})

import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Prompt

# Standalone (credentials from environment variables)
# Set AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY and AISDK_SECRET_KEY in your environment first
prompt = Prompt.get("customer-support-reply", label="production")
rendered = prompt.compile(variables={"ticket_text": "I received the wrong item."})

docs.title: create
docs.text:
create(*, name, prompt, type='text', labels=None, tags=None, config=None, model_params=None, commit_message=None, **kwargs)

Create a new prompt version via the BrowserStack public API.

Supports both 'text' and 'chat' prompt types. For chat prompts, messages are validated and normalised to the server schema. Can be called on an AISDK instance (sdk.prompt.create(...)) or as a standalone classmethod (Prompt.create(...)).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default name str

Unique name for the prompt. Must be a non-empty string.

required prompt Union[str, List[Dict[str, Any]]]

For type='text': a plain Mustache string template. For type='chat': a list of message dicts, each with at least role and content keys.

required type Optional[Literal['chat', 'text']]

Prompt type — 'text' or 'chat'. Defaults to 'text'.

'text' labels Optional[List[str]]

Labels to attach to this version (e.g. ['production']). Defaults to None (no labels).

None tags Optional[List[str]]

Tags for categorisation. Defaults to None.

None config Optional[Dict[str, Any]]

Arbitrary config dict stored alongside the prompt. Defaults to None.

None model_params Optional[ModelParams]

Optional ModelParams dict with provider and model settings — e.g. provider, model, temperature, max_tokens, top_p, top_k, stop_sequences, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty, reasoning_enabled, use_cache. Defaults to None.

None commit_message Optional[str]

Human-readable description of this version. Defaults to None.

None **kwargs

Additional keyword arguments. isActive=True is handled for backward compatibility by injecting 'production' into labels.

{}

Returns:

Type Description PromptResponse

A dict containing the created prompt's id, name, version, and related metadata.

Raises:

Type Description ValidationError

If name is empty or the prompt format is incorrect for the given type.

AuthenticationError

If the API key is invalid or expired.

TestOpsAPIError

If the API request fails.

Example 
import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

# Via AISDK instance
sdk = AISDK(
    public_key=os.environ["AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["AISDK_SECRET_KEY"],
)
result = sdk.prompt.create(
    name="customer-support-reply",
    prompt="You are a helpful support agent. Summarise the issue and reply: {{ticket_text}}",
    type="text",
    labels=["production"],
    commit_message="Initial version — plain-text reply template",
)
print(result["id"])      # e.g. "clxyz123..."
print(result["version"]) # 1

import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Prompt

# Standalone (credentials from environment variables)
# Set AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY and AISDK_SECRET_KEY in your environment first
result = Prompt.create(
    name="qa-evaluator-prompt",
    prompt="Given context: {{context}}\nAnswer: {{answer}}\nIs the answer faithful? Reply yes or no.",
    type="text",
)
print(result["name"])    # "qa-evaluator-prompt"
print(result["version"]) # 1

for p in prompts:
    print(f"{p['name']} (v{p['version']}) — labels: {p['labels']}")
# customer-support-reply (v3) — labels: ['production']
# qa-evaluator-prompt (v1) — labels: ['production']

import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Prompt

# Standalone (credentials from environment variables)
# Set AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY and AISDK_SECRET_KEY in your environment first
prompts = Prompt.list(tag="customer-support", page=1, limit=20)
for p in prompts:
    print(p["name"], "version:", p["version"])

docs.title: compile
docs.text:
Render a prompt template by substituting variables into its placeholders. Called on the prompt object returned by get().

Prompt templates use Mustache syntax: {{variable_name}}.

Parameters

Name Type Required Default Description variables dict No {} Key-value pairs substituted into the template placeholders.

Returns

  • For type='text' prompts — a rendered str with all {{variable}} placeholders replaced.
  • For type='chat' prompts — a list of message dicts with variables substituted.

Raises

  • KeyError — If a placeholder in the template has no matching key in variables.

Example — text prompt

import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

sdk = AISDK(
    public_key=os.environ["AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["AISDK_SECRET_KEY"],
)

# Fetch the production-labelled version
prompt = sdk.prompt.get("customer-support-reply", label="production")

# Render with your variables
rendered = prompt.compile(variables={"ticket_text": "My order hasn't arrived after 10 days."})
# rendered → "You are a helpful support agent. Summarise the issue and reply: My order hasn't arrived after 10 days."

# Pass the rendered string directly to your LLM
import openai
client = openai.OpenAI()
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": rendered}],
)
print(response.choices[0].message.content)

Example — chat prompt

import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

sdk = AISDK(
    public_key=os.environ["AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["AISDK_SECRET_KEY"],
)

prompt = sdk.prompt.get("qa-chat-template", type="chat", label="production")
messages = prompt.compile(variables={"question": "What is BrowserStack?"})
# messages → [{"role": "system", "content": "..."}, {"role": "user", "content": "What is BrowserStack?"}]

import openai
client = openai.OpenAI()
response = client.chat.completions.create(model="gpt-4o", messages=messages)
print(response.choices[0].message.content)

Standalone (no AISDK instance needed)

import os
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Prompt

# Set AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY and AISDK_SECRET_KEY in your environment first
prompt = Prompt.get("customer-support-reply", label="production")
rendered = prompt.compile(variables={"ticket_text": "I received a damaged product."})

docs.title: Tracing
docs.text:
Instrument your LLM application with traces, spans, generation observations, and media attachments. Two approaches are available:

  • Manual tracing — fine-grained control via AISDK instance methods.
  • Auto tracing — zero-code instrumentation via the Observe class.

docs.title: Manual Tracing
docs.text:
Create traces, spans, and generation observations using AISDK instance methods. Use sdk.trace() to start a root trace, then nest start_span(), start_generation(), or start_event() inside it.

Main client for the BrowserStack AI SDK.

AISDK extends Langfuse to add BrowserStack-specific tracing, evaluation, dataset management, experiment tracking, and prompt management capabilities. It configures OpenTelemetry trace export to the BrowserStack Evals ingestion endpoint and exposes lazy-loaded sub-clients for every product area.

Example 
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

sdk = AISDK(
    public_key="your-public-key",
    secret_key="your-secret-key",
)
span = sdk.start_span("my-step", input="hello")
span.end(output="world")
sdk.flush()
Note

TestOps is an alias for AISDK provided for backward compatibility.

docs.title: trace
docs.text:
trace(name, **kwargs)

Create a root trace and return a wrapper for attaching child spans.

A trace represents a single end-to-end request or operation. Use the returned TraceWrapper to call span() and generate() for nested child observations.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default name str

Human-readable name for the trace.

required **kwargs

Additional keyword arguments forwarded to the underlying Langfuse trace (e.g. input, output, metadata, user_id, session_id).

{}

Returns:

Type Description

A TraceWrapper object exposing span() and generate() helpers for child observations.

Example 
sdk = AISDK(public_key="pk", secret_key="sk")
t = sdk.trace("rag-pipeline", input={"query": "What is AI?"})

# Span for retrieval
retrieval = t.span("retrieval")
retrieval.end(output={"docs": ["AI is..."]})

# Generation for LLM call
gen = t.span("generation", input="docs + question")
gen.end(output="generated answer")

sdk.flush()

docs.title: start_span
docs.text:
start_span(name, expected_output=None, context=None, parent_span=None, **kwargs)

Create a span observation, optionally nested under a parent span.

Extends the base Langfuse start_span to add BrowserStack-specific expected_output and context attributes, which are stored both on the object and as OpenTelemetry span attributes.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default name str

Human-readable name for this span.

required expected_output

Ground-truth value for evaluation. Stored on the span and written as an OTEL attribute. Defaults to None.

None context

Additional context payload attached to the span. Stored and written as an OTEL attribute. Defaults to None.

None parent_span

Parent span to nest this span under. When None the span is attached to the root trace. Defaults to None.

None **kwargs

Extra keyword arguments forwarded to the underlying span API (e.g. input, output, metadata).

{}

Returns:

Type Description

Span observation object with convenience methods start_generation(), create_event(), and start_span() for building nested observations.

Example 
sdk = AISDK(public_key="pk", secret_key="sk")
span = sdk.start_span(
    "keyword-extraction",
    input={"text": "Climate change article..."},
    expected_output=["climate", "emissions"],
)
span.end(output=["climate", "environment"])

docs.title: start_generation
docs.text:
start_generation(name, model=None, prompt=None, response=None, expected_output=None, context=None, parent_span=None, **kwargs)

Create a generation observation for an LLM call.

Convenience wrapper around start_observation() that maps the familiar prompt/response/model parameters to the Langfuse input/output/model keys. Also stores BrowserStack-specific expected_output and context attributes.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default name str

Human-readable name for this generation step.

required model

LLM model name (e.g. 'gpt-4o'). Defaults to None.

None prompt

Input prompt text or dict. Mapped to input. Defaults to None.

None response

Generated response text. Mapped to output. Defaults to None.

None expected_output

Ground-truth value for evaluation scoring. Defaults to None.

None context

Additional context payload attached to the generation. Defaults to None.

None parent_span

Parent span to nest this generation under. Defaults to None (attaches to root trace).

None **kwargs

Extra keyword arguments forwarded to start_observation().

{}

Returns:

Type Description

Generation observation object with convenience methods attached.

Example 
sdk = AISDK(public_key="pk", secret_key="sk")
gen = sdk.start_generation(
    "chat-response",
    model="gpt-4o",
    prompt="What is the capital of France?",
    response="Paris",
    expected_output="Paris is the capital of France",
)
gen.end()
sdk.flush()

docs.title: start_event
docs.text:
start_event(name, expected_output=None, context=None, parent_span=None, **kwargs)

Create an event observation (a point-in-time span with no duration).

Events are lightweight observations used to mark discrete moments within a trace — for example a cache miss, a tool call, or a retrieval step that doesn't span time. Like spans they support expected_output and context for evaluation.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default name str

Human-readable name for this event.

required expected_output

Ground-truth value for evaluation scoring. Defaults to None.

None context

Additional context payload attached to the event. Defaults to None.

None parent_span

Parent span to nest this event under. Defaults to None (attaches to root trace).

None **kwargs

Extra keyword arguments forwarded to start_observation() (e.g. input, metadata).

{}

Returns:

Type Description

Event observation object with convenience methods attached.

Example 
sdk = AISDK(public_key="pk", secret_key="sk")
span = sdk.start_span("my-step")
event = sdk.start_event(
    "cache-miss",
    parent_span=span,
    input={"key": "prompt-v3"},
)
event.end()
span.end()

docs.title: flush()
docs.text:
Call sdk.flush() before your process exits to ensure all buffered spans are uploaded. Inherited from Langfuse — not specific to any one observation type.

sdk = AISDK(public_key="pk", secret_key="sk")
span = sdk.start_span("my-step", input="hello")
span.end(output="world")
sdk.flush()  # upload everything before exit

docs.title: TraceWrapper
docs.text:
Returned by sdk.trace(). Call .span() to create named child spans. The first call creates the root span; subsequent calls create nested children.

Wrapper for creating a trace with named child spans.

Returned by sdk.trace(). Call .span() to create named child spans nested under a root trace. First .span() call creates the root; subsequent calls create children.

Attributes:

Name Type Description trace_id str | None

The 32-character hex trace ID. None until first .span() call.

id str | None

The root span ID. None until first .span() call.

Example 
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK

sdk = AISDK(public_key="pk", secret_key="sk")
t = sdk.trace("rag-pipeline", user_id="user-1")

retrieval = t.span("retrieval", input="user query")
retrieval.end(output="doc chunk")

generation = t.span("generation", input="doc + query")
generation.end(output="answer")

sdk.flush()

docs.title: trace_id
docs.text:
trace_id

The trace ID of the root span as a hex string, or None if no span has been created yet.

docs.title: id
docs.text:
id

The ID of the root span, or None if no span has been created yet.

docs.title: Auto Tracing
docs.text: Zero-code instrumentation. Call Observe.init() once at startup, then every LLM call is captured automatically.

docs.title: init
docs.text:
init(*args, **kwargs)

Initialise the BrowserStack AI SDK auto-tracing system.

Must be called once before any LLM calls are made. Configures the OpenTelemetry tracer provider, attaches the TestOpsSpanProcessor to export spans, and optionally auto-instruments supported LLM libraries (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Vertex AI) and HTTP frameworks (Flask, FastAPI, Django).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default public_key str

BrowserStack public API key. Can also be set via the AISDK_PUBLIC_KEY environment variable.

required secret_key str

BrowserStack secret API key. Can also be set via the AISDK_SECRET_KEY environment variable.

required service_name str

Name of the instrumented service. Defaults to 'testops-service'.

required tracing_enabled bool

Enable OpenTelemetry tracing. Defaults to True when Observe.init is called.

required debug bool

Enable verbose debug logging. Defaults to False.

required

Returns:

Type Description

The active TestOpsTracing singleton instance, or

None if initialisation fails.

Example 
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Observe

Observe.init(
    public_key="your-public-key",
    secret_key="your-secret-key",
)

docs.title: with_trace
docs.text:
with_trace(name, fn=None, carrier=None)

Execute a function within a named trace context scope.

Creates an OpenTelemetry span that acts as the root context for all LLM calls made inside fn. The span is automatically ended when fn returns or raises. Both synchronous and async functions are supported.

For distributed tracing, pass carrier from an upstream service to automatically link this trace to the upstream trace — inject_remote_carrier() is called internally.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default name str

Name for the trace span (e.g. the function or operation name shown in the BrowserStack dashboard).

required fn

Callable to execute within the trace context. Supports both sync and async functions. Defaults to None.

None carrier

Optional trace context from an upstream service. See inject_remote_carrier() for accepted formats. Defaults to None.

None

Returns:

Type Description

The return value of fn().

Example 
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Observe

# Upstream service — start a new trace
def run_pipeline():
    Observe.set_attribute(Observe.TraceAttribute.SESSION_ID, "sess-1")
    result = openai_client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o",
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}],
    )
    return result

Observe.with_trace("run_pipeline", run_pipeline)

# Downstream service — link to upstream trace
def downstream():
    result = openai_client.chat.completions.create(...)
    return result

carrier = {"traceparent": "00-abc...", "baggage": "..."}
Observe.with_trace("downstream", downstream, carrier=carrier)

docs.title: set_attribute
docs.text:
set_attribute(key, value)

Set an attribute on the current active span.

Sets the value both as an OpenTelemetry span attribute and in the baggage context so it propagates to all child spans. Use TraceAttribute enum values for standard fields (SESSION_ID, USER_ID, INPUT, OUTPUT) or plain strings for arbitrary metadata.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default key

A TraceAttribute enum member, or a plain string for metadata. String keys are stored under AIOPS_INTERNAL.metadata.<key>.

required value

The value to set. Dicts and lists are JSON-serialised.

required Example 
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Observe

# Set standard fields using the enum
Observe.set_attribute(Observe.TraceAttribute.SESSION_ID, "sess-abc")
Observe.set_attribute(Observe.TraceAttribute.USER_ID, "user-456")

# Set arbitrary metadata using a string key
Observe.set_attribute("environment", "production")

docs.title: get_trace_id
docs.text:
get_trace_id()

Return the current active trace ID as a hex string.

Returns:

Type Description

The 32-character hex trace ID if a span is currently active,

or None if no trace is in progress.

Example 
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Observe

def my_handler():
    trace_id = Observe.get_trace_id()
    print(trace_id)  # "4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736"

docs.title: get_carrier
docs.text:
get_carrier()

Serialise the current trace context for propagation to downstream services.

Returns a dict containing traceparent and any baggage entries that can be forwarded to a downstream service as HTTP headers or a message queue payload. Pass the returned dict to inject_remote_carrier() on the receiving side.

Returns:

Type Description

dict with traceparent (str) and any active baggage

key–value pairs. Returns an empty dict if no trace is active.

Example 
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Observe

def upstream_handler():
    Observe.set_attribute(Observe.TraceAttribute.SESSION_ID, "sess-1")
    carrier = Observe.get_carrier()
    # Forward carrier to downstream service
    requests.post("http://service-b/api", json={"carrier": carrier})

docs.title: inject_remote_carrier
docs.text:
inject_remote_carrier(carrier_input)

Link this trace to an upstream service's trace context.

Call this from a downstream service (Host 2) to attach incoming spans to the upstream service's trace ID. Accepts carrier data in the W3C traceparent/baggage format.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default carrier_input

The trace context from the upstream service. Accepts:

  • dict — e.g. ``{"traceparent": "...", "baggage": "..."}
  • JSON str — same structure as dict
  • Plain W3C baggage string
required Example 
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Observe

# Downstream service — link to upstream trace
carrier = {"traceparent": "00-abc...", "baggage": "session.id=123"}
Observe.inject_remote_carrier(carrier)

# LLM calls made after this are linked to the upstream trace
result = openai_client.chat.completions.create(...)

docs.title: Media Attachments
docs.text:
Media is exported directly from browserstack_ai_sdk. Attach it as a value in any input, output, or metadata dict when creating spans or generations — the SDK uploads the binary data automatically before each trace flush and stores a compact reference string in the trace payload.

from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK, Media

sdk = AISDK(public_key="your-public-key", secret_key="your-secret-key")

span = sdk.start_span(
    "screenshot-analysis",
    input={
        "image": Media(file_path="/tmp/screen.png"),
        "question": "Are there any UI errors visible?",
    },
)
span.end(output={"analysis": "No visible errors found."})
sdk.flush()

docs.title: Creating a Media Object
docs.text:
Provide exactly one of the four creation paths:

Parameter Type Description file_path str Path to a local file. MIME type is inferred from the extension. Any file type accepted (image, audio, video, PDF, …). content_bytes bytes Raw in-memory binary data. Must also supply content_type. base64_data_uri str A complete base64 data URI: data:<mime>;base64,<data>. content_type str MIME type string, e.g. "image/png". Required with content_bytes; optional otherwise.

docs.title: From a local file
docs.text:
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Media

media = Media(file_path="/screenshots/home.png")        # image
media = Media(file_path="/recordings/user-session.mp3") # audio
media = Media(file_path="/captures/flow.mp4")           # video

docs.title: From raw bytes (must supply content_type)
docs.text:
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Media

with open("chart.jpg", "rb") as f:
    raw = f.read()

media = Media(content_bytes=raw, content_type="image/jpeg")

docs.title: From a base64 data URI
docs.text:
import base64
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Media

with open("screenshot.png", "rb") as f:
    b64 = base64.b64encode(f.read()).decode()

media = Media(base64_data_uri=f"data:image/png;base64,{b64}")

docs.title: Manual tracing (AISDK)
docs.text:
from browserstack_ai_sdk import AISDK, Media
import base64

sdk = AISDK(public_key="your-public-key", secret_key="your-secret-key")

# Vision model — attach image
gen = sdk.start_generation(
    "vision-analysis",
    model="gpt-4o",
    input={"image": Media(file_path="/tmp/chart.png"), "prompt": "Summarise this chart."},
)
gen.end(output={"summary": "The chart shows a 15% growth trend."})

# Audio transcription — from file
span = sdk.start_span("transcription", input={"audio": Media(file_path="/tmp/call.mp3")})
span.end(output={"transcript": "Hello, I need help with my order..."})

# From raw bytes
with open("recording.mp3", "rb") as f:
    raw = f.read()
span2 = sdk.start_span("audio", input={"audio": Media(content_bytes=raw, content_type="audio/mpeg")})
span2.end(output={"transcript": "Thank you for calling support."})

# From a base64 data URI
b64 = base64.b64encode(open("chart.png", "rb").read()).decode()
span3 = sdk.start_span("chart", input={"chart": Media(base64_data_uri=f"data:image/png;base64,{b64}")})
span3.end()

sdk.flush()

docs.title: Auto tracing (Observe)
docs.text:
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Observe, Media

Observe.init(public_key="your-public-key", secret_key="your-secret-key")
Observe.set_attribute("screenshot", Media(file_path="/tmp/screen.png"))

response = openai_client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Describe this screenshot."}],
)

docs.title: Supported Content Types
docs.text:
Any MIME type is accepted. Common examples:

Category MIME types Images image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp, image/gif, image/svg+xml Audio audio/mpeg (mp3), audio/wav, audio/ogg, audio/webm Video video/mp4, video/webm, video/ogg Documents application/pdf, text/plain, text/html

docs.title: TraceAttribute
docs.text:
Use these enum values with Observe.set_attribute() to attach standard metadata to the current span.

Attribute Value Use for TraceAttribute.SESSION_ID session.id Group all traces from a single user session. Pass any unique session identifier (UUID, cookie value, etc.). TraceAttribute.USER_ID user.id Associate traces with a specific end-user in your application. TraceAttribute.INPUT AIOPS_INTERNAL.trace.input Set the trace-level input shown in the BrowserStack dashboard (e.g. the user's question). TraceAttribute.OUTPUT AIOPS_INTERNAL.trace.output Set the trace-level output shown in the BrowserStack dashboard (e.g. the model's final answer). 
from browserstack_ai_sdk import Observe

Observe.set_attribute(Observe.TraceAttribute.SESSION_ID, "sess-abc123")
Observe.set_attribute(Observe.TraceAttribute.USER_ID, "user-456")
Observe.set_attribute(Observe.TraceAttribute.INPUT, "What is BrowserStack?")
Observe.set_attribute(Observe.TraceAttribute.OUTPUT, "BrowserStack is a cloud testing platform.")
